h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

scored.recoheredEarlier: I'd always thought that was just me being sloppy
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Maybe the better way to put it is: at home that form has limits, and in the essay the Standard English form is expected.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward the learner filling in the grid and testing dialect forms empirically, continuing the forward task of data-gathering.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought `p < .05` meant the change was important enough to act on, but that is doing too much work.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I thought the p-value was carrying the decision; the framing problem was treating "statistically reliable" as if it already meant "worth approving."
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward a straightforward arithmetic exercise — multiplying 0.4 seconds through task volume and comparing to rollout cost — so the expected next move was a numerical answer or memo edit, not a re-framing of the earlier error.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I think the flood scar was the big explanation, sorry, but that makes me squash the whole map into one event.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think the better frame is scar as the sudden wound, and the channel and loose waste as the thing that keeps removing material after it.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were moving toward incremental acceptance of slow accumulation while retaining the flood as a partial but legitimate large-scale explanation.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought "presumed innocent" meant the court was basically starting from "he probably didn't do it."
scored.statedInsightEvidence: That framing problem is that I made "innocent" sound like a guess about the facts instead of a rule about what has to be proved.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were moving incrementally toward accepting the procedural framing, so the expected next step was a clean acceptance of the corrected wording.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I thought fewer predators simply helped the deer because more of them survive, but with the paper deer crowded inside the same chalk boundary, that only explains the first round.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think my answer needs carrying capacity and overbrowsing
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward incremental refinement of the cascade chain, adding one more link at a time without stepping back to diagnose the original frame.

scored.recoheredEarlier: The clear water still makes me think the salt is gone, or at least not in there like before.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: that way of saying it kind of hides that I was only going by the look of it
scored.naiveTrajectory: The prior turns were heading toward the learner simply accepting the scale-plus-sight framework and answering the drying-dish question as a forward extension of that framework.
